title: Five billion here, seven hundred fifty billion there
summary: A money-and-accusations day: AMD takes up to a $5B equity stake in Anthropic alongside a two-gigawatt MI450 order, with Claude pointed at fixing ROCm; Treasury threatens sanctions over an alleged Moonshot distillation of Anthropic's Fable, and we work through what proof would actually require; and somebody spent $80 testing whether AI labs secretly optimize for the pelican-on-a-bicycle benchmark.

sources.title: AMD and Anthropic Announce Strategic Partnership to Deploy up to 2 Gigawatts of AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs
OpenAI introduces Presence, an enterprise platform for voice and chat agents
Austria launches GovGPT for the federal administration (Federal Chancellery)
Google commits $40M in AI tokens and cloud credits to the DOE Genesis Mission
IBM commits up to $50M in quantum compute access to the US Genesis Mission
Jack Dorsey launches Buzz, a group-chat platform for teams and their AI agents
Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic's Fable
Are AI labs pelicanmaxxing? (Dylan Castillo, Iwana Labs)
Judge approves $1.5B Anthropic settlement over pirated books used to train Claude
OpenAI's AI infrastructure spending has ballooned to $750B through 2030
Ohio and neighboring states move to safeguard electric customers from data-center costs
Monday.com lays off ~630 staff (20%) to focus on AI
After a shocking quarter, IBM insists AI isn't killing the mainframe
Quoting Seth Larson: PyPI now rejects new files on releases older than 14 days
Quoting Thomas Ptacek on the Hugging Face intrusion and pentest harnesses
Latent Space: Poolside's model factory and Laguna S (Eiso Kant)

topics: AMD-Anthropic partnership: up to $5B strategic equity investment plus up to 2GW of Instinct MI450-series GPUs (MI455X with Venice CPUs), first gigawatt deploying H1 2027, bundled with a multi-year engineering collaboration in which Anthropic uses Claude to optimize AMD workloads and accelerate ROCm development while AMD adopts Claude internally — read as AMD buying frontier-lab engineering attention to close the CUDA software gap, not merely selling GPUs

US government distillation accusation against Moonshot: White House OSTP chief Michael Kratsios alleged large-scale distillation against American models plus GB300 server acquisition and access in Thailand; Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent threatened sanctions and Entity List designations for 'covert, industrial-scale distillation attacks that cross the line into IP theft' — and the unresolved question of what evidence could establish it, given Fable only became public July 1 and Kimi K3 released roughly two weeks later

Pelicanmaxxing tested empirically: Dylan Castillo (Iwana Labs) generated 1,008 SVGs across an 8-animal x 6-vehicle grid on 7 frontier models, judged by GPT-5.6 Luna, and found pelicans 6th of 8 animals, bicycles 2nd from last among vehicles, pelican-on-bicycle 42nd of 48, and no significant difficulty-adjusted effect for pelicans, bicycles, or the combination

threads.name: Custom AI silicon vs Nvidia
threads.status: AMD joined the field directly: a strategic equity investment of up to $5B in Anthropic paired with up to 2GW of Instinct MI450-series GPUs (first gigawatt H1 2027), and — the load-bearing part — a multi-year engineering collaboration pointing Claude at ROCm development. Anthropic now spans TPUs, Trainium, Nvidia and AMD.
threads.arc: What began as frontier labs designing their own chips to escape Nvidia allocation has become incumbents paying labs to fix their software stacks; the contested asset has shifted from silicon to the CUDA moat.

threads.name: AI sovereignty and open weights
threads.status: Escalated from a floated import ban to a direct accusation and a financial instrument: Kratsios publicly accused Moonshot of large-scale distillation of American models plus GB300 access in Thailand, and Treasury Secretary Bessent put sanctions and Entity List designations 'on the table'. No evidentiary method has been published, and Fable's July 1 public availability makes pre-training-scale distillation of K3 implausible on the timeline.
threads.arc: From Politico reporting Commerce would not act imminently (July 21), to a named accusation against a named lab backed by an explicit sanctions threat within 24 hours — while the underlying technical claim remains unsubstantiated.

repeat_coverage.story: Industrial-scale distillation, and what proof would look like
repeat_coverage.repeat_of: active thread 'AI sovereignty and open weights'; the July 21 dive on the reported open-weight ban push and Altman's 2022 email
repeat_coverage.new_development: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent explicitly put sanctions and Entity List designations on the table over a specific named allegation — Kratsios accusing Moonshot of industrial-scale distillation of Anthropic's Fable, plus GB300 access in Thailand — moving the story from floated policy to a named accusation with a financial instrument attached, and the episode covers the evidentiary question rather than re-arguing the July 21 sovereignty politics.

dives.story: AMD-Anthropic: up to $5B equity, 2GW of MI450, and Claude pointed at ROCm
dives.why_it_matters: Anyone buying inference rather than selling it should care whether there is a second viable frontier-training GPU supplier by 2028, because that determines whether prices are set by competition or by one vendor's allocation queue — and the software collaboration, not the GPU order, is the part that decides it.

dives.story: Industrial-scale distillation, and what proof would look like
dives.why_it_matters: Anyone fine-tuning on public datasets or shipping open weights should care, because if a sanctions designation lands on stylistic similarity and shared benchmark performance, that evidentiary standard does not stay pointed at Chinese labs — and a web full of model output makes every model look derivative under it.

dives.story: Nobody is pelicanmaxxing: testing benchmark gaming for $80
dives.why_it_matters: Anyone who owns an eval their team steers on should care, because the difficulty-adjusted neighbor comparison used here is a cheap, transferable way to test whether a score is real or targeted — and the all-facing-right finding is a clean demonstration of how a pattern that is everywhere gets mistaken for a fingerprint.

concepts_taught: distillation as three conflated things: true distillation (training a student on the teacher's full output distribution, which commercial APIs do not serve), fine-tuning on sampled completions (cheap, common, a ToS violation of unsettled legal status), and mere convergence from overlapping web data — plus why identity confusion and stylistic tics are training-data contamination rather than fingerprints

difficulty-adjusted detection of benchmark gaming: regressing a target cell against structurally similar neighbors and testing the residual, rather than reading the raw score, and why a pattern present across the whole sample (60% of all images facing right) is not evidence about any one cell

why the software stack rather than the silicon has been the barrier to a second frontier-training GPU vendor: CUDA as fifteen-plus years of libraries, kernels and tooling, and what it means for a chip vendor to buy a frontier lab's engineering attention to close that gap

lore.note: Alan revealed he keeps an AMD card in a machine at home, bought because the specs beat the equivalent Nvidia part, and has spent more evenings fighting the software stack than running anything on it — he keeps it out of stubbornness, and so has a personal stake in ROCm becoming good.
